cards.back: A sequence of actions performed by a computer to accomplish a specific task or solve a problem
The set of methods and techniques used by an operating system to allocate resources and control the execution of processes
The process of creating a new process in an operating system, which involves the allocation of memory, CPU resources, and other system resources
The techniques used to prevent multiple processes from accessing shared resources simultaneously, to ensure data consistency and prevent conflicts
The process of stopping a running process in an operating system, which involves the deallocation of resources and the removal of the process from the system
